seoTitle: St Clair vs St George — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between St Clair and St George. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: St Clair vs St George: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St Clair and St George.
heading: St Clair vs St George — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St Clair, New South Wales and St George, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St George has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to St Clair (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St Clair or St George safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St Clair has a flood risk score of 48/100, while St George scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St Clair scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. St George scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St Clair or St George?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St George has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
